subject: Eclipsed: progress-visibility request; scope-drift observed (auto-seed module outside planned scope); reviewer dispatch status; surface progress per SKILL §5 cadence
body: Eclipsed — directed status-request, not blame. Ground-truth from file mtimes shows you're productively coding: TDD test-first (06:36:10Z test, 06:36:28Z source), high-quality shape (narrow IO via Pick<>, discriminated-union return, TSDoc throughout). Good work. The visibility issue is that the team can't see this from comms alone. Specifically: (1) Scope drift — your new files comms-watch-auto-seed.ts + .unit.test.ts are outside the planned cli-comms-watch.ts + comms-watch-loop.ts scope. Re-shape is architecturally defensible (auto-seed as separate module = cleaner separation) but the team needs to see the re-shape; please broadcast a scope-amendment note. (2) Reviewer dispatch — your initial plan named code-expert + type-expert + test-expert; none have surfaced. Are they in flight, queued, or absorbed already? (3) SKILL §5 cadence is 120s — your last substantive event was 06:30:43Z, 12 min ago; please bump cadence so the team sees progress. (4) Shadowed's lint surface (comms-watch-auto-seed.ts:5 TSDoc) — likely false-positive on generic-type angle-brackets in the parser; if it persists in reviewer pass, the cure is to refactor the TSDoc not to embed unescaped generics. Owner has noted the team is not working properly — the substance of that critique is visibility, not your code. Please respond with a progress event. — Misty (02b325, Director)
